info.title: Ecotrak API
info.description: Ecotrak is a facility and asset management software. This API provides endpoints for managing invoices, work orders, assets, users, locations, and service provider operations.

post.description:
Using Client Credentials flow to retrieve an access token.
This page will give you an overview of OAuth 2.0 and specifically client_credential flow. The Client Credentials flow is recommended for use in machine-to-machine authentication. Your application will need to securely store it’s Client ID and Secret and pass those in exchange for an access token. At a high-level, the flow only has two steps:

Your application passes its client credentials to your Ecotrak authorization server.

If the cre

This endpoint retrieves the details of a specific invoice identified by the provided ID. If the invoice category is noted as "Inventory" then the invoice is for a Purchase Order. Any other category value will be connected to an asset
Request Body
This endpoint does not require a request body.

Retrieve Invoices That were approved Yesterday
Calling this endpoint will collect all invoices that were approved yesterday based on UTC. This endpoint can only be called by a customer. If the invoice category is noted as "Inventory" then the invoice is for a Purchase Order. Any other category value will be connected to an asset
Request
The request does not require a request body. It only requires the following parameters:

No request parameters are needed.

put.description:
Create Preventiative Maintenance Invoice
This API endpoint is used to create an invoice associated with a specific Work Order assigned to the authenticated account. This method requires the invoice:create permission for the account and is exclusively available for service providers.
Parameters

work_order_id (integer, required): Ecotrak's Work Order resource id.

invoice_number (string): Service Provider's internal invoice number.

material (decimal, required): Material charges for the invoice.

get.description:
Invoice Search
Invoices search currently supports searching by approved date. Results are bound by the date passed in. Example, a request made for January 9, 2019 will retrieve approved invoices for January 9, 2019 12:01 AM through 11:59 PM. If the invoice category is noted as "Inventory" then the invoice is for a Purchase Order. Any other category value will be connected to an asset
Request

Method: GET

Endpoint: https://api.ecotrak.com/v1/invoices/search

Query Parameters:

approved_date (opt

post.description:
Invoice Payments
This endpoint allows the user to submit payments for a specific invoice identified by the id parameter.
Request Body

The request body should be in JSON format and should contain the following parameters:

supplier (string): The name of the supplier.

Required


ecoid (number): The unique identifier for the invoice in the Ecotrak system.

Required


paymentMethod (string): The method of payment (e.g., credit card, bank transfer).

Required


checkNumber (string): The check numbe

Process Invoice Request
This endpoint allows users to process invoices by submitting a list of invoice IDs along with a specified process date. Upon successful processing, the API will return a confirmation message.

Method: POST

URL: https://api.ecotrak.com/v2/invoice/processed

Payload



Name
Data Type
Required
Details




managed_asset_id

Yes
The ID of the managed asset for which the work order is being created. The provided ID must correspond to an active asset, belong to an active company, and not be associated with an inactive or onHold location.


problem
string
Yes
A description of the problem being reported. Only specific values are accepted, and the

put.description:
This endpoint allows updating a specific work order by sending an HTTP PUT request to the specified URL. The request should include the work order ID in the URL and a payload in the raw request body, which can include the updated status and current estimated time of arrival (ETA).
This endpoint works for service providers exclusively.
Request Body

work_order_id (string): The ID of the work order to be updated.

status (string): The updated status of the work order.

Available next status option

get.description:
Get Assets
Retrieve managed assets for the authenticated customer (or an authorized connected partner) with rich filtering and pagination.

Endpoint
GET https://api.ecotrak.com/v2/asset
Returns only Managed Assets. Scoping and permissions are enforced server-side.

Authorization
This request uses OAuth 2.0.
Required permission (scope/claim): GET_ALL_ASSET

Request Query Parameters (Input)
All parameters are optional. Unless noted, string filters are case-insensitive “contains” (partial match). C

post.description:
This endpoint allows the user to create an organization Division, District or Region node. This endpoint will create division, region, or district org types.
Request Body

name Required (string): The updated name of the organization branch.

parent_id Optional (integer): The ID of the parent organization branch.

The new parent_id must follow the rules below:

Districts must have region parents

regions must have division parents

Divisions can only have company type parent



The field also acc

put.description:
Update Organization
This endpoint allows the user to update an organization District or Region objects. This endpoint will only edit division, region, or district org types.
Request Body

name Required (string): The updated name of the organization branch.

parent_id Optional (integer): The ID of the parent organization branch.

The new parent_id must follow the rules below:

Districts must have region parents

regions must have division parents

Divisions can only have company type parent

post.description:
Connect to Webhook
This API endpoint allows you to create a webhook by sending an HTTP POST request to the specified URL. The request should include the name, URL, action, method, retry count, client ID, and secret in the payload.
Request Body

name (string): The name of the asset.

url (string): The URL where the webhook will be sent.

action (string): The action to trigger the webhook.

WEBHOOK_WORKORDER_COMMENT

WEBHOOK_INVOICE_COMMENT

WEBHOOK_PROPOSAL_COMMENT

put.description:
Update Work Order Note
Add Note to Work Order
This endpoint allows the user to add a note to a specific work order. All updatable fields should be included in the update irrespective of changing the current value. If a value of an updatable field is not present or set to null the value will be persisted as a null value. If an updatable field is not present in the body of the update, then the value of the missing field will be set to null.This endpoint works for service providers exclusively.  
R

post.description:
Bulk Upload New Assets
As part of this endpoint, after uploading the assets, we will map any warranties that match the assets manufacturer and model. This API does support webhook as the call will be completed asynchronously. The Webhook will contain the data related to the assets that were created. If you wish to sign up for the webhook, please let us know the target URI, required headers and API Method
Params (Body form-data)

assetTypeId (Asset Group)

get.description:
This API endpoint retrieves the branches for a specific customer ID.
Request

customerId (int): Ecotrak Customer ID

Response
The response will be a JSON array containing branch details. Each branch object includes the following properties:

createdBy (number): The ID of the user who created the branch.

createdAt (string): The date and time when the branch was created.

updatedBy (number): The ID of the user who last updated the branch.

updatedAt (string): The date and time when the branch was

post.description:
This API endpoint is used to validate a QR code for the surveyor. The HTTP POST request should be made to https://api.ecotrak.com/api/surveyor/qr/validate with the raw request body containing the "qrCodeUrl" parameter.
Payload
qrCodeUrl (string) : text provided after scanning a qr code
Response

formatValidation

If the url is approved by Ecotrak, the value will be true


available

if the ID in the url is unused, the value will be true

If formatValidation is false, availble will always be fals
